what_it_does: Defines a standalone HTML probe page that loads UI styles and scripts, creates a sample corpus record, turns it into a unit with display metadata, neighbours, and stats, then appends the rendered unit into the page mount.
observations: The page links Google Fonts plus local piece.css and phone.css stylesheets.
The body contains only a #mount element for rendered output.
Inline script defines a REC capture object with lineage, digest, kind, projection, and source_address fields.
The unit data includes title, line, card title, three card lines, six neighbours, and three stats.
Neighbours include role, label, score, and address fields.
The rendered output is produced by DNA.unitFromCorpus followed by DNA.renderUnit.
standouts: The file is a focused render probe for a connected unit constellation.
The fixture combines corpus-derived data with editorial display copy and neighbour metadata.
The comment text explicitly distinguishes the real corpus record from the neighbour constellation fixture.
conventions: Uses a minimal static HTML probe structure with local UI dependencies and inline fixture data.
Keeps styling outside the file except for a small body rule.
Uses const fixtures and appends the rendered result after script dependencies load.
Uses abbreviated code://… addresses for neighbour entries.
concerns: The probe depends on global DNA APIs without local guards or fallback messaging.
The fixture uses shortened neighbour addresses, so the addresses are display-like rather than complete source locations.
External font loading can affect visual consistency if the network is unavailable.
notes: A static render fixture for inspecting how one corpus-derived unit and its neighbour constellation appear in the unit UI.
questions: Are the shortened neighbour addresses intended only for display in this probe?
Should the score values be treated as visual prominence, recall metadata, or both?
Is the REC object meant to stay embedded here or mirror a generated fixture format?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated renderUnit probe purpose matches the actual behavior: the file builds a sample unit with neighbours and renders it into the page.
apparent_themes: editorial metadata layered onto source data
intention_signals: It seems intended to provide a small, inspectable page for checking how a real-looking captured code unit renders with surrounding neighbour context and summary stats.
novelty: The fixture treats related files as an editorial constellation with roles, scores, and re-drillable addresses rather than as ordinary import links.
contribution: Contributes a concrete static fixture for rendering a corpus-derived unit with display copy, neighbour roles, prominence scores, source-style addresses, and summary stats in one local probe page.
summary_for_embedding: This is a static HTML render probe that constructs a sample captured code unit with neighbour constellation metadata and mounts DNA.renderUnit output into the page.
intention_for_embedding: It was for visually exercising the unit rendering path with a realistic corpus record, editorial copy, linked neighbours, and compact stats.
